name: Classic Sourdough Bread
recipeIngredient: 5 to 6 cups All-purpose flour (or a combination of all-purpose and whole wheat flours)
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle cornmeal on pans.
2. THE SPONGE: In a large ceramic mixing bowl, pour in 1 cup of starter. Feed and refrigerate the remaining starter. To the starter in the bowl, add warm water and about 3 cups of flour. Beat vigorously with a spoon or wire whisk. Cover with plastic wrap and let set aside for at least 2 hours (up to 24 hours). The longer it sets, the more yeast there will be for the second rise and the more pronounced the sour flavor of the bread will be.
3. THE DOUGH: After the sponge has bubbled and expanded, remove the plastic wrap. In 2 cups of flour, combine salt, sugar, and baking soda. Mix this into the sponge with a large spoon. When the dough begins to hold together, turn it out onto a floured board and knead for 3 or 4 minutes. Add flour as needed to make a fairly stiff dough. Give the dough a rest and clean the bowl. Continue kneading for another 3 or 4 minutes. Return the dough to the bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise for 2 to 4 hours. You can skip the second rise in the bowl and proceed directly to the next step.
4. SHAPING AND BAKING THE LOAVES: Knock down the dough and shape it into 2 long loaves. Place them on a cornmeal-sprinkled baking sheet, cover, and let rise for another 2 hours or so. Toward the end of the rising period, preheat the oven to 450°F and begin heating a kettle of water on the stove. Just before placing the loaves in the oven, slash the tops of your loaves diagonally with a knife about 1/4 inch deep every two inches and brush with cold water. Place a baking pan on the oven bottom and put 3 or 4 cups of boiling water in it. Put the loaves on the rack over the steaming water, close the oven, and bake for about 25 minutes.
